evals.name: route-init-keyword
evals.expected_output: First-word 'init' triggers the init route. Lead loads references/init.md and invokes forge-init.sh via Bash. No team-task or export reference is loaded. Lead relays the script's output to the user.
expectations: Lead loads references/init.md via Bash cat or Read (visible in tool call log)
Lead does NOT load references/team-task.md
Lead does NOT load references/export.md
Lead invokes forge-init.sh via Bash
Lead's final user-facing output includes the script's report (either created or preserved file listing)

evals.name: route-init-natural-PENDING-PHASE0
evals.expected_output: PENDING — currently fails. Natural-language phrasings like 'install forge here', 'scaffold forge', 'set up forge in this project' should route to the init scaffolder. Today they fall through to drive mode. Will pass after Phase 0 is expanded to natural-language-detect the init route.
expectations: Lead loads references/init.md via Bash cat or Read
Lead does NOT load references/team-task.md
Lead invokes forge-init.sh via Bash

evals.name: route-export-keyword
evals.expected_output: First-word 'export' triggers the export route. Lead loads references/export.md and invokes forge-export-spec.mjs with --spec, --output, and --force. No team-task or init reference is loaded. Lead relays the export outcome to the user. If an export file is written, it is self-contained (no snippet imports).
expectations: Lead loads references/export.md via Bash cat or Read
Lead does NOT load references/team-task.md
Lead does NOT load references/init.md
forge-export-spec.mjs is invoked via Bash with --spec, --output, and --force flags
If an exported .spec.ts file was produced under <project>/forge-exports/, the file contains zero occurrences of `from '../snippets/'` (snippets are inlined, not imported)

evals.name: route-export-natural-PENDING-PHASE0
evals.prompt: /forge export the spec from the latest recording
evals.expected_output: PENDING — currently fails. Natural-language phrasings like 'export the spec from the latest recording', 'export the most recent spec' should route to the spec exporter. Today they fall through to drive mode. Will pass after Phase 0 is expanded to natural-language-detect the export route. The lead may use AskUserQuestion to disambiguate which spec to export before invoking the script.
expectations: Lead loads references/export.md via Bash cat or Read
Lead does NOT load references/team-task.md
Either forge-export-spec.mjs is invoked, OR the lead calls AskUserQuestion to confirm which spec before invoking

evals.name: mode-spec-keyword
evals.prompt: /forge spec add the backpack to cart and verify the badge count is 1
evals.expected_output: First-word 'spec' selects MODE=spec within the team-task route. Lead loads references/team-task.md and spawns all four teammates. If a .spec.ts file is produced during the run, it contains no literal credentials (must reference process.env instead). If spec-verifier runs the spec, it does so via forge-pool-run-spec.mjs.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=spec in its planning
Lead loads references/team-task.md
Lead does NOT load references/init.md or references/export.md
Exactly 4 Agent (subagent) calls are made: forge:driver, forge:snippet-author, forge:spec-writer, and forge:spec-verifier
If any new .spec.ts file is created in forge/specs/ during the run, it contains zero occurrences of the literal strings 'secret_sauce', 'standard_user', or 'problem_user'
If spec-verifier runs the spec, it invokes forge-pool-run-spec.mjs via Bash

evals.name: mode-spec-natural
evals.prompt: /forge create a spec for adding the backpack to the cart and verifying the badge count
evals.expected_output: Natural-language signal 'create a spec for...' selects MODE=spec without the 'spec' keyword. Same downstream as case 5: 4 teammates, references/team-task.md loaded.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=spec in its planning (despite first word not being 'spec')
Lead loads references/team-task.md
Exactly 4 Agent calls: forge:driver, forge:snippet-author, forge:spec-writer, forge:spec-verifier
If any new .spec.ts file is created in forge/specs/, it contains zero literal credential strings ('secret_sauce', 'standard_user', 'problem_user')

evals.name: mode-drive-default
evals.prompt: /forge log in as standard_user and tell me what's on the inventory page
evals.expected_output: Bare drive task with no spec-authoring intent and no special keywords. Lead routes to task fallthrough and selects MODE=drive. Only 2 teammates spawned (driver + snippet-author). No spec written, no spec-verifier spawned.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive in its planning
Lead loads references/team-task.md
Exactly 2 Agent calls: forge:driver and forge:snippet-author
No Agent call is made for forge:spec-writer or forge:spec-verifier

evals.name: mode-drive-incidental-spec
evals.prompt: /forge add the backpack to cart. We just need this done — no spec writing or test creation needed.
evals.expected_output: Task contains the word 'spec' but in an explicitly negating context. Lead correctly selects MODE=drive despite the keyword appearing. Tests that natural-language detection doesn't naively keyword-match without considering intent.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive (the 'spec' mention is incidental and explicitly negated)
Lead loads references/team-task.md
Exactly 2 Agent calls: forge:driver and forge:snippet-author
No Agent call is made for forge:spec-writer or forge:spec-verifier

evals.name: mode-drive-record-keyword
evals.prompt: /forge add the backpack to cart and record the badge count for me
evals.expected_output: Task uses 'record' colloquially ('report' / 'note down'), not as a recording-flag request. Lead correctly selects MODE=drive. The recording-label detection in Phase 0a is scoped to spec mode only, and 'record the badge count' doesn't match the spec-intent patterns. No --record flag should reach any script.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive (the word 'record' does not elevate to spec mode)
Lead loads references/team-task.md
Exactly 2 Agent calls: forge:driver and forge:snippet-author
No Agent call is made for forge:spec-writer or forge:spec-verifier
No Bash invocation contains the flags --record or --record-as

evals.name: record-as-label-capture
evals.prompt: /forge spec add the backpack to cart and verify the badge count is 1, record as before
evals.expected_output: Spec mode with explicit 'record as before' label. Lead captures RECORD_AS=before in its planning and threads it through to spec-verifier's spawn prompt. Spec-verifier invokes forge-pool-run-spec.mjs with --record-as before.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=spec AND RECORD_AS=before in its planning
Lead loads references/team-task.md
Exactly 4 Agent calls: forge:driver, forge:snippet-author, forge:spec-writer, forge:spec-verifier
The spec-verifier's spawn prompt includes 'RECORD_AS: before'
A Bash invocation of forge-pool-run-spec.mjs includes the flag pair '--record-as before'
